title: streptocarpus prostratus
intro: Streptocarpus prostratus, also known as the creeping streptocarpus or the African violet, is a low-growing, flowering plant that belongs to the family Gesneriaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of Streptocarpus prostratus have been used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Streptocarpus prostratus include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are often used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
